result:
Done — the spec is fully implemented and the project builds cleanly (0 warnings, 0 errors) on the first of the 10 allowed build cycles.

What I changed in `src/TempConvert.calr`:

- **Refactor:** added a private helper `ClampToCeiling(value, ceiling)` (`§F{f000:...:priv}`) that holds the single clamping implementation, with its own postcondition `result <= ceiling`. The three existing conversions now bind their working variable via `§C{ClampToCeiling} §A input §A <ceiling> §/C` instead of each repeating the mutable-binding-plus-`§IF` clamp pattern. All existing preconditions (`§Q`) and postconditions (`§S`) are unchanged, so every declared input-domain and output-range guarantee is preserved exactly.
- **New function:** `KelvinToCelsius` (`§F{f004:...:pub}`) clamps to 5273 through the same helper and returns `k - 273`, with the spec's contracts declared: precondition `k >= 0`, postconditions `result >= -273` and `result <= 5000`.

I verified the generated C# in `obj/calor/`: the module exposes the four required public static functions (`CelsiusToFahrenheit`, `CelsiusToKelvin`, `FahrenheitToCelsius`, `KelvinToCelsius`), the arithmetic is identical to the original (32-bit integer, truncating division), and the clamping is now shared through one private helper with runtime contract checks intact.
